comment_text.sn2.5:1.2: Dāmali is elsewhere unknown. But cf. Vedic dāman, “giver”, which is associated with magha at Rig Veda 5.79.4. Given its close association with Indra, who is “made to be a giver” (8.93.8, see too 5.36.1, 6.24.4), it seems likely this is yet another epithet of Sakka.
comment_text.sn2.5:2.1: Dāmali is treating the “brahmin” as one who still needs to practice. | This line parallels the opening of the Mettasutta (Snp 1.8:1.1).
comment_text.sn2.5:3.1: For the Buddha, the only true brahmin is the arahant.
comment_text.sn2.5:3.5: This couplet confirms the meaning “swim” for āyūhati.

root_text.sn2.5:1.2: Atha kho dāmali devaputto abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇo kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn2.5:1.3: Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho dāmali devaputto bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.5:1.2: Then, late at night, the glorious godling Dāmali, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side,
translation_text.sn2.5:1.3: and spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.5:2.1: “This is what should be done by a brahmin:
translation_text.sn2.5:2.3: Then, with the giving up of sensual pleasures,
translation_text.sn2.5:2.4: they won’t hope to be reborn.”
translation_text.sn2.5:3.1: “The brahmin has nothing left to do,”
translation_text.sn2.5:3.4: So long as a personage fails to gain a footing in the river,
translation_text.sn2.5:3.6: But someone who has gained a footing <j>and stands on dry land
translation_text.sn2.5:3.7: need not swim, <j>for they have reached the far shore.
translation_text.sn2.5:4.1: Dāmali, this is a simile for the brahmin,
translation_text.sn2.5:4.2: alert, a meditator who has ended defilements.
translation_text.sn2.5:4.3: Since they’ve reached the end of rebirth and death,
translation_text.sn2.5:4.4: they need not swim, <j>for they have reached the far shore.”

variant_text.sn2.5:3.7: sova → soti (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr); hoti (sya-all, km); so (?)

reference_text.sn2.5:3.1: cck15.63, csp1ed12.45, csp2ed12.45, ms12S1_385, pts-vp-pli1ed1.48, sya15.66
